text:
AI Developer Radar｜Daily｜2026-05-26

今日结论：
扫描到 100 个候选项目，AI 相关候选 95 个。 今日 50 个项目达到 24h stars >= 50。 当前最强信号集中在 Coding Agent / SWE Agent。

今日精选项目
1. Lum1104/Understand-Anything
   GitHub: https://github.com/Lum1104/Understand-Anything
   Category: Coding Agent / SWE Agent
   Stars: 32,180 (24h +4931, 7d 暂无基线)
   Score: 77 | Risk: Low
   One-liner: Turn any codebase, knowledge base, or docs into an interactive knowledge graph you can explore, search, and ask questions about.
   Why worth watching: It addresses a universal pain point for developers onboarding to new codebases, has broad platform support (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, Copilot, Gemini CLI, etc.), and offers a practical, visual approach to code comprehension that goes beyond static analysis.
   Developer takeaway: A powerful plugin that integrates with major AI coding tools to provide instant codebase understanding via knowledge graphs, semantic search, diff impact analysis, and guided tours. It can significantly reduce onboarding time and improve code navigation.
   Problem solved: Developers struggle to understand large, unfamiliar codebases. This tool provides a multi-agent pipeline that analyzes code, builds a knowledge graph of files, functions, classes, and dependencies, and offers an interactive dashboard for visual exploration, search, and guided tours.
   Target users: Developers, especially those joining new teams or working with large codebases; technical leads; and anyone using AI coding assistants who wants deeper insight into their project structure.
   Risk notes: The README content is untrusted; claims about features and platform compatibility should be verified. The project is relatively new (pushed May 2026) and may have evolving stability. Relies on LLM for semantic analysis, which can introduce variability.
   LLM confidence: high

2. rohitg00/ai-engineering-from-scratch
   GitHub: https://github.com/rohitg00/ai-engineering-from-scratch
   Category: AI Agent Framework
   Stars: 19,158 (24h +2441, 7d 暂无基线)
   Score: 74 | Risk: Low
   One-liner: A free, open-source, 435-lesson curriculum that teaches AI engineering from math foundations to production agents, with every lesson producing a reusable artifact.
   Why worth watching: Massive star growth (2,441 stars in 24h) driven by the demand for practical, hands-on AI education that goes beyond API wrappers and produces real artifacts.
   Developer takeaway: Offers a complete, self-paced curriculum with code in Python, TypeScript, Rust, and Julia, where each lesson builds a reusable prompt, skill, agent, or MCP server.
   Problem solved: Bridges the gap between using AI tools and understanding how they work internally, providing a structured, end-to-end path from linear algebra to autonomous swarms.
   Target users: Developers and engineers who can already code and want to deeply understand AI—from math to deployment—rather than just call APIs.
   Risk notes: README content is untrusted; the curriculum is large (~320 hours) and may not be fully completed or maintained. Verify lesson quality before relying on it.
   LLM confidence: high

3. esengine/DeepSeek-Reasonix
   GitHub: https://github.com/esengine/DeepSeek-Reasonix
   Category: Coding Agent / SWE Agent
   Stars: 8,890 (24h +1922, 7d 暂无基线)
   Score: 73 | Risk: Low
   One-liner: A DeepSeek-native AI coding agent for the terminal, optimized for prefix-cache stability to minimize token costs during long sessions.
   Why worth watching: Exceptional cost efficiency (99.82% cache hit, ~$12 vs ~$61 for 435M tokens) and rapid adoption (8.9k stars, +1.9k in 24h) driven by DeepSeek's popularity and practical developer tooling.
   Developer takeaway: Offers a specialized, high-efficiency coding agent for DeepSeek users, with unique cache optimization, but is locked to DeepSeek and has 199 open issues indicating active development.
   Problem solved: Reduces API costs for AI-assisted coding by maximizing cache hit rates on DeepSeek's prefix-cache mechanism, enabling affordable long-running agent sessions.
   Target users: Developers using DeepSeek for AI-assisted coding who want to minimize API costs and run long-lived agent sessions in the terminal.
   Risk notes: DeepSeek-only (no model flexibility), 199 open issues suggest potential instability, and reliance on DeepSeek's prefix-cache mechanic may be affected by API changes.
   LLM confidence: high

4. mukul975/Anthropic-Cybersecurity-Skills
   GitHub: https://github.com/mukul975/Anthropic-Cybersecurity-Skills
   Category: Coding Agent / SWE Agent
   Stars: 9,447 (24h +907, 7d 暂无基线)
   Score: 71 | Risk: Low
   One-liner: A structured library of 754 cybersecurity skills for AI agents, mapped to five industry frameworks (MITRE ATT&CK, NIST CSF, etc.) and compatible with 20+ AI coding platforms.
   Why worth watching: It addresses the cybersecurity workforce gap (4.8M unfilled roles) by enabling AI agents to handle security tasks, and it's the largest open-source skills library with cross-framework mappings, gaining rapid adoption (9.4k stars, 907 stars in 24h).
   Developer takeaway: Developers can integrate these skills into any agentskills.io-compatible platform (Claude Code, Copilot, Cursor, etc.) to give their AI agents expert cybersecurity capabilities without manual training or custom tooling.
   Problem solved: AI agents lack structured domain knowledge to perform cybersecurity tasks like a senior analyst. This repo provides step-by-step playbooks that agents can execute, bridging the gap between generic LLMs and expert-level security workflows.
   Target users: Security engineers, DevSecOps teams, AI agent developers, and cybersecurity professionals who want to automate or augment security analysis with AI.
   Risk notes: The README contains promotional links (surveys, playground) and is a community project not affiliated with Anthropic. Skills are static and may become outdated; users should verify against current threats.
   LLM confidence: high

5. affaan-m/ECC
   GitHub: https://github.com/affaan-m/ECC
   Category: MCP / Tool Calling
   Stars: 192,845 (24h +1769, 7d 暂无基线)
   Score: 67 | Risk: Low
   One-liner: ECC is a harness-native operator system that optimizes AI agent performance through skills, instincts, memory, security scanning, and research-first development for tools like Claude Code, Codex, and Cursor.
   Why worth watching: With 182K+ stars and rapid daily growth (1,769 stars in 24h), it addresses the high demand for practical agent tooling, winning an Anthropic hackathon and offering a comprehensive, cross-harness solution.
   Developer takeaway: Developers get a complete, open-source system to enhance AI agent workflows with token optimization, memory persistence, continuous learning, and security scanning, saving months of custom configuration.
   Problem solved: It solves the lack of a unified, production-ready system for configuring, optimizing, and securing AI agent harnesses, reducing manual setup and improving agent reliability and efficiency.
   Target users: Developers and teams using AI coding agents (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, etc.) who need production-grade performance, security, and cross-harness compatibility.
   Risk notes: The README is promotional and may overstate capabilities; the project is complex with many components, and the Rust alpha (ECC 2.0) is experimental. Verify actual functionality before relying on it.
   LLM confidence: high

6. manaflow-ai/cmux
   GitHub: https://github.com/manaflow-ai/cmux
   Category: AI Agent Framework
   Stars: 19,599 (24h +478, 7d 暂无基线)
   Score: 67 | Risk: Low
   One-liner: A native macOS terminal built on Ghostty with vertical tabs, notification rings, and an in-app browser, designed for AI coding agents like Claude Code and Codex.
   Why worth watching: It directly addresses the pain of running many AI coding agents simultaneously, a common workflow for developers using tools like Claude Code, Codex, and Gemini. The project is native (Swift/AppKit, not Electron), highly performant, and integrates deeply with Ghostty. Its rapid star growth (19.6k stars, +478 in 24h) reflects strong community interest in better terminal UX for AI-assisted development.
   Developer takeaway: cmux is a composable, scriptable terminal+browser that gives developers primitives (not a locked-in workflow) to manage AI agents. It supports custom hooks, session restore for many agent CLIs, and a socket API for automation. The project is open-source and actively developed, with nightly builds and a Discord community.
   Problem solved: Managing multiple parallel AI coding agent sessions in a terminal is chaotic—standard terminals lack agent-aware notifications, workspace context (git branch, PR status, ports), and a scriptable browser for agent interaction. cmux provides a native, GPU-accelerated terminal with a sidebar that surfaces agent notifications and workspace metadata, plus a built-in browser agents can control.
   Target users: Developers who run multiple AI coding agents (Claude Code, Codex, OpenCode, etc.) in parallel on macOS and want a native, fast terminal with agent-aware notifications, workspace management, and a scriptable browser.
   Risk notes: The project is relatively new (first release likely recent) and has a high number of open issues (2129) relative to its size, which may indicate rapid growth outpacing bug fixes or a large feature backlog. It is macOS-only and depends on Ghostty. The README is promotional and may oversell stability.
   LLM confidence: high

7. nexu-io/open-design
   GitHub: https://github.com/nexu-io/open-design
   Category: Coding Agent / SWE Agent
   Stars: 52,292 (24h +849, 7d 暂无基线)
   Score: 66 | Risk: Low
   One-liner: Open-source, local-first alternative to Claude Design that turns 16 coding-agent CLIs into a design engine with 132 skills and 150 design systems.
   Why worth watching: It's a viral open-source response to a popular but locked-down product (Claude Design), gaining 40k+ stars in two weeks by offering freedom, local execution, and compatibility with many AI agents.
   Developer takeaway: A powerful, extensible design prototyping tool that leverages existing AI coding agents, supports multiple export formats, and is fully customizable via skills and design systems.
   Problem solved: Claude Design is closed-source, cloud-only, and locked to Anthropic's model. Open Design provides a BYOK, local-first, open-source alternative that works with any major coding agent CLI or API proxy.
   Target users: Developers, designers, and product builders who want an open, local, and agent-agnostic design prototyping tool as an alternative to Claude Design or Figma.
   Risk notes: Early preview (0.8.0-preview) with rapid iteration; may have bugs, breaking changes, or incomplete features. Relies on external CLI agents and local setup complexity.
   LLM confidence: high

8. sickn33/antigravity-awesome-skills
   GitHub: https://github.com/sickn33/antigravity-awesome-skills
   Category: MCP / Tool Calling
   Stars: 38,716 (24h +125, 7d 暂无基线)
   Score: 65 | Risk: Low
   One-liner: An installable library of 1,465+ reusable SKILL.md playbooks for AI coding assistants like Claude Code, Cursor, Codex CLI, and Gemini CLI.
   Why worth watching: Massive star count (38k+) driven by the rapid adoption of AI coding assistants and the need for ready-to-use, curated skill libraries that work across multiple tools.
   Developer takeaway: Offers a quick way to enhance AI coding workflows with pre-built skills for planning, coding, debugging, security, and more, with a simple npm installer and tool-specific setup.
   Problem solved: Eliminates the need to collect and manage one-off prompt snippets by providing a searchable, installable catalog of structured agentic skills for recurring development tasks.
   Target users: Developers using Claude Code, Cursor, Codex CLI, Gemini CLI, Antigravity, Kiro, OpenCode, or GitHub Copilot who want to improve AI agent effectiveness with structured playbooks.
   Risk notes: README content is user-provided and may contain inaccuracies; the project's large scope may lead to variable skill quality and maintenance challenges.
   LLM confidence: high

9. ruvnet/ruflo
   GitHub: https://github.com/ruvnet/ruflo
   Category: AI Agent Framework
   Stars: 55,157 (24h +310, 7d 暂无基线)
   Score: 63 | Risk: Low
   One-liner: Ruflo is a multi-agent orchestration platform for Claude Code that enables coordinated swarms of 100+ specialized AI agents with self-learning memory, federation, and enterprise security.
   Why worth watching: It has extremely high star counts (55k+) and daily star growth (310), likely due to its comprehensive plugin ecosystem, integration with popular tools like Claude Code and Codex, and the growing demand for multi-agent orchestration in AI-assisted development.
   Developer takeaway: Ruflo provides a production-ready, extensible framework for building and coordinating multi-agent AI workflows, with a rich plugin system covering orchestration, memory, intelligence, testing, security, and more, all accessible via CLI or Claude Code plugins.
   Problem solved: It solves the coordination and memory limitations of single-agent AI coding assistants by allowing agents to self-organize into swarms, learn from past tasks, remember across sessions, and securely communicate across machines, all while the developer continues coding normally.
   Target users: Developers and teams using Claude Code or similar AI coding assistants who need to scale beyond single-agent interactions, manage complex workflows, and integrate AI agents into their development pipeline with enterprise-grade security and federation.
   Risk notes: The README contains promotional language and unverifiable claims (e.g., '22.2M+ ecosystem downloads', '115k git clones 14d') that may be inflated. The project has a high number of open issues (564) relative to forks, which could indicate maintenance challenges or rapid feature development. The description mentions 'self-learning swarm intelligence' and 'enterprise-grade architecture' but actual capabilities need verification.
   LLM confidence: medium

10. code-yeongyu/oh-my-openagent
   GitHub: https://github.com/code-yeongyu/oh-my-openagent
   Category: AI Agent Framework
   Stars: 59,509 (24h +214, 7d 暂无基线)
   Score: 63 | Risk: Low
   One-liner: An open-source multi-agent orchestration harness that unifies Claude Code, Codex, and other AI agents into a single terminal-based workflow.
   Why worth watching: Extreme popularity (59k stars) driven by viral testimonials claiming it replaces paid tools like Cursor, its 'ultrawork' one-command simplicity, and the controversy around Anthropic blocking OpenCode due to this project.
   Developer takeaway: A practical, community-driven tool that dramatically accelerates development by automating multi-agent orchestration; worth trying for any developer using AI coding assistants.
   Problem solved: Eliminates the friction of juggling multiple AI coding agents and configuring workflows by providing a unified harness that orchestrates agents in parallel, with features like team mode, intent analysis, and hash-anchored edits.
   Target users: Developers and teams using multiple AI coding agents (Claude Code, Codex, etc.) who want a unified, powerful terminal-based workflow to automate complex tasks.
   Risk notes: README is promotional and may exaggerate claims; project is in active refactoring (multi-harness OS) with 615 open issues; license is SUL-1.0 (non-standard); anonymous telemetry enabled by default.
   LLM confidence: high

数据说明：
- GitHub API 只提供当前 stars，总量变化来自本项目保存的历史 snapshot。
- daily delta 使用约 24h 前 snapshot，weekly delta 使用约 7 天前 snapshot。
- Potential Score 为规则评分，用于排序，不代表项目质量结论。
